message:
refactor(pager): serialize freelist into write set instead of direct I/O

Rename persist_freelist_to_disk to serialize_freelist_to_write_set and
change it to accumulate freelist trunk pages and page-1 header updates
into the caller's write_set HashMap rather than issuing direct
db_file.write() calls and cache evictions.

This decouples freelist serialization from immediate I/O, allowing the
commit path to batch all page writes (including freelist updates) into
a single WAL append or direct-write pass. Benefits:
- Eliminates redundant I/O round-trips during commit.
- Avoids cache evictions that could race with concurrent readers.
- Uses page pool buffers (cache.pool().acquire()) instead of heap
  Vec allocations for trunk pages.
- When page 1 is already in the write set (e.g., from header
  patching), the freelist update merges into the existing buffer
  rather than doing a separate read-modify-write cycle.

Also hoists max_written computation above the page-1 borrow so the
header patching block does not need a mutable reborrow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
